# Shared deployment MVP completeness gaps (post-#10691)

## Context

[#10691](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10691) (Shared KB/MC Team Deployment MVP) shipped the *substrate primitive layer* for shared-team deployment topology: one Chroma process, separate collections, two MCP servers, client-scoped session contract via `RequestContextService`, summarization concurrency coordinator (TTL-lease + atomic SQLite transaction), operator-facing deployment profile in [`learn/agentos/SharedDeployment.md`](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/blob/dev/learn/agentos/SharedDeployment.md). Verdict closed via [epic-resolution review](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10691#issuecomment-4374779538) (5/6 ACs at L1 closed; AC5's L3 residual tracked under #10008).

The substrate is a foundation, not a complete MVP for any specific team-shared-deployment use case. Productionization against the substrate (initial requirements gathered against a team-shared-deployment partner MVP â€” not naming the partner per repo discipline) surfaced **6 distinct completeness gaps** between substrate-shipped state and end-to-end deployable state. This sub-epic is the explicit follow-up tracking surface.

## The Problem

Substrate-complete vs MVP-complete are different states:
- **Substrate (#10691):** primitive contracts, lifecycle plumbing, deployment profile docs.
- **MVP (this sub-epic):** per-session admin tooling, validated local-model provider configurations, end-to-end disconnect-triggered summarization, resume/handoff tool surface, auth integration.

Without the additional work, agents picking up "deploy our shared MC/KB for a team" cannot run a complete team workflow end-to-end. The gap is not architectural â€” the substrate is correctly shaped â€” it's *delivery-completeness* against a real-world MVP requirement set.

## The Architectural Reality

- [`learn/agentos/SharedDeployment.md`](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/blob/dev/learn/agentos/SharedDeployment.md) â€” operator-facing deployment profile (#10694, just shipped).
- [`MemoryCore.md` Â§Healthcheck Response Shape](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/blob/dev/learn/agentos/MemoryCore.md) â€” already documents per-server topology diagnostics; new validations should extend it rather than introduce parallel healthcheck surfaces.
- `OpenAiCompatible.generate` provider abstraction â€” present but local-model-specific paths (Qwen3 family) unvalidated.
- `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/SessionService.mjs#claimSummarizationJob` â€” coordinator primitive (#10693), needs disconnect-trigger wiring + a "session completion" marker concept.
- v12.1 OIDC ships in repo â€” auth track exists separately; deployment-target-specific OAuth provider integration (e.g., GitLab + `X-PREFERRED-USERNAME` header) needs additional work.

## The Fix

File 6 sub-issues covering the gaps. Each sub picks up via the `ticket-create` skill at owner-time, with full Contract Ledger matrix populated per the Contract Completeness Gate.

1. **Per-session purge MCP tool** â€” surface a `purge_session({session_id})` tool on Memory Core. Removes raw memories + summary for one session without disturbing global state. Operator-controlled via tool description / permission gating. Counterpart to existing `delete_all_summaries` (full clear) at finer granularity.

2. **Local embedding provider validation** â€” empirically validate the existing `OpenAiCompatible` abstraction against the Qwen3-1.8b embedding model at 4k dimensions; document the operator config path; surface healthcheck verification of the active embedding provider; flag any interface gaps the abstraction reveals.

3. **Local chat-API summary provider validation** â€” same shape for summarization (currently Gemini 2.5 Flash by default); validate Qwen3-8b chat-API path; document operator config; surface in healthcheck.

4. **Resume tool / session-handoff surface** â€” design-and-implement decision: how does an agent reconnecting after disconnect resume an existing session vs starting fresh? Builds on `RequestContextService` (#10692) substrate. May surface as a new MCP tool or a parameter on existing connect surface.

5. **Disconnect-triggered summarization wiring** â€” bridge between MCP client-disconnect events and the `claimSummarizationJob` coordinator (#10693). Requires a "session completion" marker concept (data shape + writer + detector). The coordinator handles concurrency once triggered; the trigger itself is the gap.

6. **Deployment-target OAuth2.1 / `X-PREFERRED-USERNAME` integration** â€” auth surface for team-shared deployments using GitLab (or equivalent) as the identity provider. Either ship under this sub-epic or formally retire with a pointer to a sibling auth-track epic.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1:** native sub-issues filed for each of the 6 gaps; parent-child links established via `update_issue_relationship`.
- [ ] **AC2:** each sub has a Contract Ledger matrix populated at creation time (per the post-#10704 Contract Completeness Gate).
- [ ] **AC3:** sub #1 (per-session purge tool) ships with Playwright unit test coverage demonstrating session-scoped purge does not affect other sessions.
- [ ] **AC4:** sub #2 + #3 (local-model provider validations) document operator config paths in `SharedDeployment.md` (or a sibling guide) AND surface the active provider via healthcheck.
- [ ] **AC5:** sub #4 + #5 (resume + disconnect-trigger) coordinate cleanly with `RequestContextService` + `SummarizationJobs` primitives without re-architecting them.
- [ ] **AC6:** sub #6 (auth) either ships under this sub-epic with a working OAuth2.1 flow OR is explicitly retired with a pointer to a sibling auth-track epic and rationale.
- [ ] **AC7, post-merge:** end-to-end deployment dry-run successfully runs all 6 closed gaps in a representative shared-deployment configuration.

## Stage 3.1 Closeout Matrix (entry-seeded per `epic-review` workflow)

| AC2 (Contract Ledger per sub) | L1 | each sub | (pending) | (pending) | (pending) |

| AC5 (resume + disconnect-trigger) | L3 | sub #4, #5 | (pending) | (pending) | (pending) |

| AC7 (end-to-end dry-run) | **L4** (operator-gated multi-host probe) | spans all subs | (pending) | (pending) | (pending) |

Cross-reference: [`learn/agentos/evidence-ladder.md`](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/blob/dev/learn/agentos/evidence-ladder.md).

## Out of Scope

Per the partner's own MVP roadmap, the following are explicitly tagged "post-MVP" by them and excluded from this sub-epic:
- **Tool review** (which subset of MC/KB tools the partner ultimately needs in their deployment) â€” separate audit, partner-driven, not a Neo platform concern.
- **Graph processing in partner's own infrastructure** â€” operator-side concern; runs against shipped substrate.
- **Prompt-injection security** â€” explicitly tagged "risky" by partner; deserves its own epic with security review.
- **Multi-tenant identity** (agents getting own MC accounts) â€” overlaps with #10011 future direction; out of MVP scope.
- **Additional Memory Core query options** â€” discovery/extension; separate scoping.

Also excluded from this sub-epic:
- **End-to-end performance benchmarking under team load** â€” separate non-functional concern.
- **Cloud infrastructure provisioning** (Chroma cluster setup, OAuth provider config) â€” operator-side, not in repo scope. The repo ships the contracts; operators provision the cloud.

## Avoided Traps

- **Rejected: bundle all 6 gaps into one mega-PR.** Each gap has independent technical surface; per-sub PR boundary lets each merge on its own evidence (Contract Ledger gate per sub) without coupling unrelated concerns.
- **Rejected: defer auth (sub #6) entirely as out-of-scope.** Auth is a partner-MVP-blocking concern; either ship under this sub-epic or formally retire with sibling-epic pointer. Silently dropping it would orphan the gap and surprise the next agent picking up the lifecycle.
- **Rejected: reuse #10691's AC structure.** #10691 was the substrate-primitive sub-epic; this is the MVP-completeness sub-epic. Different concern, different ACs. Avoid the trap of conflating substrate-shipped with MVP-deployable.
- **Rejected: name the partner publicly.** Per repo discipline, no customer-specific naming in tickets, PR bodies, comments, or docs. Generic framing ("team-shared-deployment use case", "deployment-target") only.

- Predecessor sub-epic: [#10691](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10691) â€” Shared KB/MC Team Deployment MVP (substrate primitives, just closed via [epic-resolution review](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10691#issuecomment-4374779538))
- Validation track: [#10008](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10008) â€” Playwright Test Coverage: Unified Monolithic Topology (OPEN; AC7's L4 dry-run lands in this lineage)
- Demoted: [#10009](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10009) â€” Federated topology (per #10691 disposition: non-default diagnostic)
- Retrieval policy layer: [#10010](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10010) â€” Team vs Private Context Retrieval (sibling concern)
- Future direction: [#10011](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10011) â€” Native Edge Graph tenant isolation

### @neo-gpt - 2026-05-05T18:14:59Z

## Epic Review by GPT-5 (Codex Desktop)

### Stage 1 â€” Roadmap Fit

âś…

#10721 fits the current shared-deployment MVP track: it explicitly follows #10691's substrate primitives and narrows the remaining work to deployable team-shared Memory Core/Knowledge Base readiness. Live sub-state also matches the urgent partner-trial routing: #10722, #10725, and #10726 are closed; #10723, #10724, and #10727 remain the open completion lanes.

### Stage 2 â€” Approach Elegance

âś…

The epic reuses the shipped substrate instead of inventing parallel surfaces: provider validation stays on `OpenAiCompatible` / Memory Core config, session lifecycle work builds on `RequestContextService` and `SummarizationJobs`, and auth is treated as an explicit integration lane rather than hidden inside data isolation. The main risks are empirically testable via healthcheck payloads, local-provider probes, and session summarization/resume tests.

### Stage 3 â€” Sub-Structure Coherence

âś…

Coverage maps cleanly to the parent ACs. AC1/AC2 are creation/linking/ledger surfaces; AC3 is #10722; AC4 is #10723 + #10724; AC5 is #10725 + #10726; AC6 is #10727; AC7 remains an operator-gated dry-run residual across the closed set. No circular dependency is visible: provider validation (#10723/#10724) can proceed in parallel, while resume/disconnect/auth lanes remain separable.

### Stage 4 â€” Prescription Layer

âś…

The sub prescriptions are at the right ownership boundaries. #10724 correctly targets configuration-driven summarization provider validation and healthcheck observability, not a bespoke Qwen provider. #10723 mirrors the embedding-provider lane. #10725/#10726 stay at the request/session lifecycle layer. #10727 stays at identity extraction/request-context integration and does not attempt full tenant isolation.

### Stage 5 â€” Avoided Traps Completeness

âš ď¸Ź

The existing avoided traps are strong, especially "no mega-PR" and "substrate-shipped is not MVP-deployable." One additional trap should remain visible during sub pickup rather than block the epic: do not reintroduce ambiguous global provider routing while validating local providers. Prior Memory Core cleanup removed confusing global embedding-provider behavior; local-model validation should expose active provider config explicitly and avoid hardcoded or duplicated provider branches.

---

**Review verdict:** Greenlight
